## Acceptance Criteria

Pass. REQ-09 is satisfied across the ai-review prompt copies. The reviewer is now instructed to emit a single numbered enumeration of every finding across all categories plus an explicit total count in:

- `scripts/code-review-prompt.md:76`
- `scripts/ai-review.sh:82`
- `contrib/gates/prompts/code-review.md:37`
- `contrib/gates/ai-review.sh:82`

The wording also states that findings may not be withheld for later rounds, which directly addresses the issue description.

## Architecture And Separation Of Concerns

Pass. The change is confined to prompt text and shell-script prompt assembly. No Rust domain, storage, command, CLI, or output boundary is modified.

## Functional Paradigm

Pass. No production Rust logic changed.

## Correctness And Invariants

Pass. No file-write, event logging, gate-transition, assignee/label parsing, or PID-handling code changed. The verdict parser remains untouched.

## Safety

Pass. No unsafe code introduced.

## Testing

Pass. `run_history` is empty, so there is no failed test evidence to act on. I did not run cargo tests; this is a prompt-only change and there is no succeeded cargo-ci gate in the provided issue context. I did run the required repository validation: `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed.

## Error Handling

Pass. No fallible Rust code or error paths changed.

## CLI Contract

Pass. No CLI command behavior or `--json` surface changed.

## Documentation

Pass. No public Rust API was added or changed. The updated prompt text is clear and duplicated consistently across the active and contrib ai-review copies.

## Prior Review Feedback

Pass. `run_history` is empty, so there is no prior review feedback to verify.

## Jit Issue Management

Pass. The prerequisite issue `7f709152` is marked `done` locally and in the provided context. `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed.

No findings.
